response.justification: Bogdan brings rare geospatial domain expertise and proven distributed systems thinking from high-throughput infrastructure work, making him interesting for a routing-focused role. However, his C++ experience is over a decade old (2014–2015) with no Rust background, creating a significant gap for a senior IC position where C++ is the primary requirement. His recent career trajectory shifted toward web/full-stack development, though his consistent ability to master diverse tech stacks suggests he could ramp up—at non-trivial cost.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain knowledge from GISCollective and Green Map work, directly relevant to routing tiling
Proven distributed-systems thinking from Dunnhumby's high-throughput microservices handling millions of requests per second
Consistent track record of learning and mastering diverse programming languages across multiple domains over 15+ years
concerns: C++ experience is 11+ years old; no Rust experience despite being listed as an acceptable alternative
Career focused on web/full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) for past 6+ years, away from systems-level programming
Geospatial experience is web-application and data-collection focused, not routing infrastructure or data-pipeline optimization

key_factors.factor: Dated C++ with no Rust alternative
Shift from systems to web development
